signals.title: USD/CAD — Geopolitical Shock + Oil Triangle Break
signals.text: WTI Oil +3.71% at $99.0 following the collapse of US-Iran peace talks and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. USD/CAD at 1.3670 remains pinned despite the crude surge as Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% (+150bp) carry advantage favors USD. CAD COT LF net short -46,889 (long%=26.4%) reflects heavy speculative bearishness. If oil sustains a break above $100, CAD short-covering risk intensifies regardless of the carry deficit.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence + RBNZ
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5948 testing the 0.5950 level. COT LF net short -19,294 (long% at extreme 18.5%) shows specs aggressively fading the RBNZ's hawkish bias (Market bias: Hike). This creates a high-conviction short-squeeze setup if the RBNZ reaffirms tightening at the 27 May meeting. Fed 3.75% vs RBNZ 2.25% (+150bp) carry currently supports USD, but extreme positioning limits further downside.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7240. COT LF net long +58,994 (long% 78.3%) indicates heavy institutional backing, while retail sentiment is 89% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is unsuccessfully fading the institutional trend. RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75% (-60bp) carry favors AUD. Retail capitulation risk provides fuel for an extension toward 0.7300 if China car sales data (down 21.6%) is offset by energy-driven PPI beats.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + ECB Stance
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.715 vs norm of -0.115 (z=+1.74σ). The pair is moving in lockstep with European equities rather than traditional rate differentials. ECB's de Guindos downplaying energy shock risks suggests a steady hand at the 11 Jun meeting. Fed 3.75% vs ECB 2.0% (+175bp) carry keeps the structural ceiling on EUR/USD at 1.1800. VIX at 18.2 (normal volatility) suggests cross-asset stress is not yet forcing a JPY/CHF safe-haven rotation.
